other_observers: Stephen Saunders (by happenstance); Scott Severs was the original observer 3 or 4 years ago. This year I am uncertain of the original observer.   Independent viewers include Cade Cropper, Cathy Sheeter, Steve Mlodinow, Dan Brookes, and Cheryl Teuton. Others may exist as well, but these are the ones I knew of from eBird.
specific_location: Teller Farms trailhead between the parking lot and the pedestrian crossing on Valmont. Both on the south and north sides of the road. Started on the south side, then moved to the north side in the abandoned building lot.
description: Similarly sized and shaped to the White-crowned Sparrows around it. Noted as different (even without seeing the head pattern) due to its grayer overall plumage coloration. Showed darker gray on the nape and auriculars. Head pattern was distinctive with a yellow forecrown and black lores, eyestripe and supraloral feathers. Noted no white in the crown pattern even though this is noted in field guides. Face of this bird seemed dirtier than photos I have seen online almost like the bird was significantly worn. Yellow on the forecrown was not as bright as in field guides almost showing more of a dull dandelion or goldenrod color instead of bright like a buttercup (I guess I have flowers on the mind). Rest of body looked like a White-crowned Sparrow aside from the darker overall cast. Beak was dark gray to black depending on lighting, but didn't show the bi-colored expected in field guides. At no point did the beak look yellow, pink, or orange like a White-crowned Sparrow.Behaviors: Worked the roadside and weedy patches around the trail by hopping along with White-crowned Sparrows. Occasionally would post up after being flushed, but wouldn't remain up for very long.Call: None notedPlumage: worn adult
